title: Lemon meringue pie
subtitle: Sharp lemon curd filling with a soft meringue top in a shortcrust case
excerpt: Lemon meringue pie: a blind-baked shortcrust case filled with a sharp, cornflour-thickened lemon curd, topped with a soft meringue and baked until the peaks are toasted. The filling is intensely lemon-forward; the meringue adds sweetness and contrast.
sourceNotes: Lemon meringue pie as a combination of lemon curd filling and meringue top in a pastry case is documented in American domestic cookery from the mid-nineteenth century, with British adaptations following shortly after. Mrs Beeton's later editions document lemon and meringue pie variants. The modern cornflour-thickened lemon curd filling under a soft French meringue is the canonical twentieth-century form, widely reproduced in British and American domestic baking references. The recipe is fully public-domain.

recipe.makeAheadNotes: The filling can be made the day before and refrigerated. Add the meringue on the day and bake.
recipe.temperatureNote: fan oven for blind-bake; 160°C fan for setting the meringue

baking.bakeTemperatureNote: fan oven, 20 to 25 minutes until meringue is golden at the peaks

glossaryTerms.definition: Spreading meringue onto a hot lemon filling, rather than a cold one, helps the two layers bond and reduces the risk of weeping (the liquid that pools between filling and meringue). The heat from the filling starts to cook the underside of the meringue before it goes in the oven, which is important because the oven temperature is low — around 160°C fan. Spread the meringue while the filling is still warm, sealing it right to the pastry rim so there are no gaps for liquid to pool.

content.text: Lemon meringue pie has three components, each with its own technique. The good news: all three are straightforward in isolation. Keep the lemon filling sharp — it should taste aggressively of lemon before the meringue goes on, because the meringue brings considerable sweetness.

Roll the {{shortcrust-pastry}} to 3 mm and line the 23 cm tart tin. Refrigerate for 30 minutes. Preheat the oven to 180°C fan. Line with baking paper and baking beans and blind-bake for 15 minutes. Remove the paper and beans and bake for a further 5 minutes. Set aside, oven still on at 160°C fan.

Whisk the {{cornflour}} and {{caster-sugar}} (filling portion) together in a saucepan. Stir in the juice of the {{lemon}} (about 120 ml) and 300 ml water. Cook over medium heat, stirring constantly, until thickened and bubbling. Remove from the heat. Beat in the {{egg-yolks}} one at a time, then stir in the {{unsalted-butter}} and the lemon zest. Pour into the warm case.
Whip and apply the meringue, bake 20–25 minutes
Whisk the {{egg-whites}} and {{cream-of-tartar}} to stiff peaks. Gradually add the {{caster-sugar}} (meringue portion), whisking until thick and glossy. Read the
note: spread the meringue onto the warm filling immediately, sealing to the pastry rim. Bake at 160°C fan for 20 to 25 minutes until golden at the peaks. Cool to room temperature before slicing.
